name: Madagascar

colors.symbolism: purity; together with red, the colours of the historic Merina monarchy that ruled Madagascar before colonisation
sovereignty; the colour of the historic Merina kingdom
the coastal Hova peasantry and the country's hope and progress

description: A white vertical band at the hoist, one-third of the flag's width; at the fly, two equal horizontal stripes — red over green — taking the remaining two-thirds. The white forms an L-shape against the red-and-green bicolour at the fly.
history: Adopted on 14 October 1958, when Madagascar became an autonomous republic within the French Community ahead of full independence on 26 June 1960. The colours combine the red and white of the pre-colonial Merina kingdom (which ruled most of Madagascar in the 19th century) with green, traditionally associated with the coastal Hova peasantry. The flag has been retained unchanged through all subsequent constitutional changes, including the 1975–1992 Marxist Democratic Republic.

features.symbolism: a white vertical band at the hoist taking the inner third, with two equal horizontal stripes (red over green) on the outer two-thirds; the colours echo the historic Merina kingdom (red and white) and the Malagasy peasantry (green)
